mechanism_narrative: A low and stable unemployment rate, currently at 4.1%, indicates a healthy and robust labor market. This fosters strong consumer confidence and spending, which in turn supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat on average when UNRATE is below 5% over any 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable federal funds rate, currently at 1.15%, signifies an accommodative monetary policy stance. This reduces the cost of capital for businesses and makes equities more attractive relative to fixed-income assets, thereby driving S&P 500 returns higher.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat on average when FEDFUNDS is consistently below 2.0% over any 5-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with robust housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand and investment activity. This indicates a healthy economic expansion that directly boosts corporate revenues and overall S&P 500 performance.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% MoM AND HOUST 3-month average > 1100k units
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 486553.0 and HOUST at 1127.0 are strong
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are negative or flat when both RSAFS 3-month average growth is positive and HOUST 3-month average is above 1100k units for 3 consecutive months, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production in an environment of moderate inflation suggests healthy manufacturing activity without overheating. This leads to strong corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns, as the economy expands sustainably.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month average growth > 0.2% MoM AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth between 1.5% and 2.5%
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are negative despite INDPRO 3-month average growth exceeding 0.2% MoM when CPIAUCSL YoY growth is between 1.5% and 2.5% over any 5-year period, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS), indicates a robust labor market and strong consumer purchasing power. This directly supports corporate earnings and overall S&P 500 performance over the short term.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day returns are negative on average when PAYEMS 3-month average growth exceeds 150k over any 2-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent GDP growth reflects a healthy and expanding economy, providing a favorable backdrop for corporate profitability and investor confidence. This fundamental economic strength leads to sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are negative or flat on average when GDPC1 YoY growth is above 2% over any 5-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Low inflation combined with a low federal funds rate creates an ideal environment for equity valuations. Real interest rates are low, and future earnings are discounted at a lower rate, significantly boosting S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth < 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS < 1.5% for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 246.639 and FEDFUNDS at 1.15 are low
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are negative when CPIAUCSL YoY growth is below 2.5% and FEDFUNDS is below 1.5% for 6 consecutive months, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A very low unemployment rate, while generally positive, can signal an overheating economy if accompanied by rising inflation. This scenario often prompts Federal Reserve tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day returns are positive on average when UNRATE is below 4.5% and CPIAUCSL YoY growth is above 2.5% over any 2-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.
